3253:- **Portable Schema**: Migrations written in DB-agnostic SQL (no SQLite-specific syntax).
3280:**Pillar 2: Portable Schema & Migrations [CX-DBP-011]**
3518:#### 2.3.13.7 Loom Storage Trait + Portable Schema (Example) [ADD v02.130]
3522:[ADD v02.156] LoomBlock/LoomEdge records, LoomViewFilters, LoomSearchFilters, LoomBlockSearchResult, and LoomSourceAnchor are canonical portable backend library contracts. Their meaning MUST survive SQLite-now / PostgreSQL-ready storage, export, and replay instead of being hidden behind view-only adapters.

item.text: The self-review found two concrete cleanup items worth fixing before validator feedback: `api/loom.rs` and `storage/loom.rs` are still formatting-only noise, and the sorted-view group query still applies `limit/offset` before filtering. I’m tightening both now without widening scope.

I found a validator-grade gap and I’m closing it with evidence, not just reasoning: adding a sorted-view pagination assertion that would have failed under the old group-selection logic, then rerunning the scoped verification after reverting the format-only noise.

item.text:
**Self-Review**
I found one concrete defect in the prior Loom patch: sorted Loom views could still apply `limit/offset` before filter-qualified groups were selected, which could hide valid groups under filtered pagination. I fixed that in [sqlite.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/sqlite.rs#L2407) and [postgres.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs#L1980), and I strengthened the evidence in [storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L573) with a paged sorted-view assertion at [storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L1059) that would have failed under the old behavior. I also removed formatter-only drift from `src/api/loom.rs` and `src/storage/loom.rs`, so they are clean again.

Current code diff is limited to these packet-scoped Loom files: [loom_fs.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/loom_fs.rs#L41), [postgres.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs#L331), [sqlite.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/sqlite.rs#L2407), [storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L573), and [storage_conformance.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/storage_conformance.rs#L32). No out-of-scope source files were reintroduced.

**Verification**
Commands run:
